serviceNotes.engine oil: BMW Condition Based Service (CBS) sets engine oil and filter replacement from the service display based on driving conditions, cold starts, and fuel quality—not a single fixed km figure. A 30,000 km mfr row models the typical maximum distance before the display calls for an oil service on many EU models; follow the vehicle display and annual minimums. garage.wiki BMW E90 interval tables list oil as "according to service display".
serviceNotes.brake fluid: Brake fluid is hygroscopic and absorbs moisture over time, reducing its boiling point and braking effectiveness. Replacement is time-based, not distance-based — typically every 2 years regardless of mileage.
serviceNotes.spark plugs: garage.wiki BMW maintenance tables tie engine air filter, fuel filter (diesel), and spark plugs (petrol) to "with each third engine oil change". At a ~30,000 km CBS oil cadence this implies roughly 90,000 km; this platform uses 90,000 km as the mfr-aligned distance. Confirm with your service display and owner's literature.
serviceNotes.fuel filter: garage.wiki BMW maintenance tables tie engine air filter, fuel filter (diesel), and spark plugs (petrol) to "with each third engine oil change". At a ~30,000 km CBS oil cadence this implies roughly 90,000 km; this platform uses 90,000 km as the mfr-aligned distance. Confirm with your service display and owner's literature. Diesel fuel filter applies to diesel engines.
serviceNotes.gearbox fluid: Automatic and manual gear oil is often labelled lifetime; many specialists recommend exchange around 100,000 km for long-term health.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: Coolant inhibitor effectiveness declines with age; preventive full exchange around 120,000 km is a common workshop recommendation.
serviceNotes.intake carbon clean: Direct-injection turbo engines can accumulate intake valve deposits; walnut or chemical cleaning around 120,000 km restores airflow.
serviceNotes.micro filter: Cabin (micro) filter replacement is driven by the service display on CBS vehicles; 30,000 km is a typical planning interval when aligning with major services.
serviceNotes.air cleaner element: garage.wiki BMW maintenance tables tie engine air filter, fuel filter (diesel), and spark plugs (petrol) to "with each third engine oil change". At a ~30,000 km CBS oil cadence this implies roughly 90,000 km; this platform uses 90,000 km as the mfr-aligned distance. Confirm with your service display and owner's literature.
serviceNotes.check front brake pads: Front brake pad thickness is monitored via CBS wear sensors and inspection at scheduled services; 30,000 km is a reasonable inspection cadence between major services.
serviceNotes.check rear brake pads: Rear brake pad thickness is monitored via CBS wear sensors and inspection at scheduled services; 30,000 km is a reasonable inspection cadence between major services.
serviceNotes.vehicle check: BMW CBS scheduled inspection. A comprehensive check covering braking systems, vehicle diagnostics, and technical components.
